name: Lasagne with Turkey and Fresh Tomato Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the sauce, core the tomatoes and cut them into 1-inch cubes. Put the tomatoes in a food processor and blend until coarsely chopped (about 4 cups).
2. Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a skillet; add 2 tablespoons garlic. Cook briefly but do not brown.
3. Add the tomatoes, red pepper flakes, 1 teaspoon fresh oregano, and salt and pepper to taste. Bring to a boil and simmer for 10 minutes.
4. To prepare the lasagne, heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a non-stick skillet; add the turkey. Cook, stirring to break up the meat, until lightly browned.
5. Add 1 teaspoon garlic, stirring; add wine. Bring to a boil over high heat and cook until the wine evaporates.
6. Add the tomato sauce, 1 teaspoon oregano, and salt and pepper to taste. Bring to a boil and simmer for 5 minutes.
7. Meanwhile, cook the lasagne in salted water according to package instructions, adding the lasagne strips one at a time. Cook until tender, add cold water to cool, and drain. Spread the strips one at a time on a damp cloth.
8. Lightly grease a 2-quart oblong baking dish. Add a layer of lasagne.
9. Beat the ricotta with hot water to make it spreadable. Spread about 1/3 of the ricotta over the lasagne.
10. Spread a layer of meat sauce over the ricotta and sprinkle about 1/4 of Parmesan cheese on top.
11. Continue making layers, ending with a layer of lasagne. Sprinkle with the remaining Parmesan cheese.
12. Pour melted butter over all.
13. Bake in a preheated 400-degree oven for 15 minutes or until the lasagne is piping hot and bubbling. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
